key_drivers: US-Iran War Escalation (VIX spiked +12.39% to 23.76; WTI surged ~8.7% intraday on supply disruption premium; safe-haven bid for JPY)
Rising Real Yields (DFII10 at 1.80%, +15bps; MOVE index +25.68% month; compressing equity risk premiums across all index futures)
USD Strengthening (DXY +1.5% week to 99.04; weighing on commodities, risk FX, and equity valuations globally)
BoJ Hawkish Pivot (Ueda calls March/April meetings 'live'; narrowing JPY-USD differential supports 6J BULLISH)
China Slowdown (official PMI 49.0, 2nd consecutive contraction; HY OAS widening to 2.97%; RTY and 6A most exposed)

fallbacks_used: DXY level sourced from Yahoo Finance/MarketWatch (TradingView dynamic rendering unavailable)
Gold ETF flows sourced from World Gold Council monthly reports and State Street March 2026 Monthly Gold Monitor
WTI crude price sourced from FT.com and CNBC real-time feeds
overnight_changes: VIX spiked +12.39% to 23.76 (risk-off territory, well above 20 threshold)
WTI crude surged ~8.7% intraday to $81.15 (US-Iran war supply disruption premium)
Gold opened higher at $5,155-5,168 then pulled back to $5,069-5,085 (dollar headwind limiting upside)
Silver fell -2.60% to $81.02 (underperforming gold in risk-off)
MOVE index rose +6.42% to 74.53 (bond market volatility surging)
SOX fell -1.17% to 7,821 (-6.26% week from ATH of 8,498 on Feb 25)
EIA crude inventories: +3.5M barrels to 439.3M (9-month high, bearish supply)

imminent: US-Iran War ongoing (active military conflict, Strait of Hormuz risk, Iran response awaited)
FOMC March 18-19, 2026 (13 days away, 97.3% hold probability)
ECB March 18-19, 2026 (ING: rate cut off table due to Middle East war)
near_term: RBA March 16-17, 2026 (possible rate hike; cash rate at 3.85%, CBA expects May hike)
BoJ March 18-19, 2026 (Ueda called meeting 'live' for potential hike)
EIA Weekly Petroleum Report March 11, 2026 (next inventory data)
background: OPEC+ output increase 206k bpd from April 2026 (partially offset by Iran disruption)
China 2026 GDP target 4.5-5% (lowest in years; AUD and copper headwind)
HY OAS widening trend (2.97%; small-cap and credit stress building)
